rationale: Several references investigate the use of trehalose as a cryoprotectant for mammalian cells, including human cell lines, and compare it with or combine it with DMSO. Reference [0] studies sucrose and trehalose with DMSO in cryopreservation of HEP-G2 cells, a human tumor line, which is similar but not identical to HeLa cells. Reference [1] explores trehalose-mediated cryopreservation without DMSO in human cells, addressing intracellular delivery of trehalose. Reference [3] develops DMSO-free cryoprotectants including trehalose and sucrose for Jurkat cells, another human cell line. None of the references explicitly test replacing sucrose with trehalose alone as a cryoprotectant in HeLa cells or measure a 15 percentage point increase in post-thaw viability compared to 10% DMSO, nor do they specifically measure intracellular ice formation reduction as the mechanism. Thus, the exact experiment is not found, but closely related work on trehalose versus DMSO and sucrose exists.

references.title: Cryopreservation of HEP-G2 cells attached to substrates: the benefit of sucrose and trehalose in combination with dimethyl sulfoxide.
references.snippet:
